2605.08303.title: GNN for Structural Displacement Prediction
2605.08303.paper_summary: Accurate prediction of structural displacements under external loading is fundamental to structural health monitoring and seismic safety assessment. Although the finite element method (FEM) remains the prevailing approach because of its high accuracy, its considerable computational cost restricts its suitability for real-time monitoring applications. To address this limitation, this study proposes a data-driven framework based on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), in which structural systems are represented as graphs with joints modeled as nodes and structural members as edges. By incorporating both geometric and mechanical properties into the graph representation, the proposed model learns the relationship between applied loads and structural responses directly from simulated data. A synthetic dataset was generated from a two-story frame structure using ANSYS, and both a conventional Neural Network (NN) and a GNN were trained for comparison. The results show that the proposed GNN framework predicts displacements and rotations with high accuracy and outperforms the NN model, demonstrating its potential as a fast and efficient alternative to traditional FEM-based analysis.
2605.08303.paper_summary_zh: 準確預測在外部載荷下的結構位移對於結構健康監測和地震安全評估至關重要。<lb/>儘管有限元方法（FEM）因其高精度而仍然是主流方法，但其相當高的計算成本限制了其在實時監測應用中的適用性。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，本研究提出了一種基於圖神經網絡（GNN）的數據驅動框架，其中結構系統被表示為圖，接頭被建模為節點，結構成員則作為邊。<lb/>通過將幾何和機械特性納入圖的表示，所提出的模型從模擬數據中直接學習施加載荷與結構響應之間的關係。<lb/>使用ANSYS從一個兩層框架結構生成了合成數據集，並訓練了傳統神經網絡（NN）和GNN進行比較。<lb/>結果顯示，所提出的GNN框架以高精度預測位移和旋轉，並且表現優於NN模型，顯示出其作為傳統FEM基礎分析的快速高效替代方案的潛力。
2605.08303.author: Hung-Fu Chang et.al.
2605.08303.authors: Hung-Fu Chang, Tzu-Kang Lin, Yung-Li Cheng
